title: daemon/store: set_message_reaction uses read-modify-write on reactions JSON — unbounded key injection
description: set_message_reaction (store.rs line 1856) reads reactions JSON, deserializes to a Map, inserts reaction_id as a key, and writes back. The reaction_id is caller-supplied and has no length or count validation. A caller can add an unbounded number of reaction_id keys to a single message's reactions column, growing it without bound. At 1000 reactions × 100-byte keys = 100 KB of reactions JSON per message; at the JMAP layer there is no cap on how many set_message_reaction calls can be made per message. The reactions JSON blob is loaded into RAM on every read. Trigger: client calls MessageReaction/set repeatedly for the same message. Bad behavior: reactions JSON grows without bound; every message read allocates proportionally more RAM.
